name: J&P Check Cashing - Chicago Fullerton Ave
description_short: Order Express is a money transfer and check-cashing service operating 70+ branches across 25 states since 1993, serving Latino immigrant communities with multilingual service.
description_long:
Order Express Inc. was founded in 1993 as a proudly Mexican company governed by US laws and statutes. The first branch opened in Chicago, Illinois, targeting the city's large Latino immigrant population with a focus on efficient money transfer services. As of January 2021, the company expanded to become a regulated operator in 25 states with over 70 company-owned branches and authorized agents, establishing itself as a significant player in the alternative financial services sector.

The Chicago Fullerton Ave location offers a comprehensive suite of financial services beyond basic check cashing. Core services include check cashing, money transfer, money orders, bill payments, and currency exchange. The branch also provides ancillary services such as plane ticket sales, Bitcoin ATM access, courier services, vehicle-related services (renewals and plates), phone top-up, and bus passes. Hours are Monday–Friday 9 am–7 pm, Saturday 9 am–5 pm, and Sunday 9 am–4 pm.

Order Express distinguishes itself through bilingual service (English and Spanish), a stated commitment to speed and safety in money transfers, and a broad service menu designed for immigrant communities. The company maintains a loyalty program and holds a 4.9-star Google rating with customer testimonials emphasizing good service and staff attentiveness. The business model reflects deep market understanding of Latino immigrant needs, combining remittance services with everyday financial tasks.

The primary limitation is that Order Express remains a niche financial services provider focused on check cashing and money transfer rather than credit-building or loan products. Customers should understand that while the company offers convenience and multilingual support, it does not provide credit products, personal loans, or credit repair services. The service is best suited for individuals needing immediate check-cashing, money transfer, or bill payment solutions rather than those seeking traditional banking relationships.
pros: Operates 70+ branches across 25 regulated states with long establishment since 1993
Bilingual (English/Spanish) service specifically designed for Latino immigrant communities
Extended hours (9 am–7 pm weekdays, 9 am–5 pm Saturday) for working customers
4.9-star Google rating with documented customer satisfaction on service quality
Diverse service menu beyond check cashing: money transfer, bill payment, currency exchange, Bitcoin ATM, courier services
Money transfer positioned as quick and safe with focus on sending to loved ones
Loyalty program available for repeat customers
cons: No credit-building products or personal loan offerings for financial advancement
Limited to check cashing and alternative financial services; not a traditional bank
Specific fee structures for check cashing and money transfer not disclosed on website
No information provided about verification requirements or documentation needed
As a niche provider, may have less consumer protection infrastructure than larger banks
best_for: Latino immigrants and Spanish-speaking communities needing multilingual financial services
Individuals without traditional bank accounts seeking check cashing and money transfer
Customers sending remittances to family in Mexico or other countries
People needing immediate bill payment or currency exchange services
diagnosis: Order Express is best for Spanish-speaking individuals and Latino immigrants needing check cashing, money transfer, and bill payment services with extended hours and multilingual staff. The main caveat is that this is a specialized alternative financial services provider, not a credit-building resource—customers should not expect personal loans, credit cards, or credit repair services.
